market.question: Will Northlight Harbor suspend outbound traffic before 18:00 UTC on November 12?
market.description: A fictional binary market about whether the harbor authority will halt all outbound vessel movements before the stated cutoff.
market.resolutionRules: Resolve SUSPENDED if the fictional Northlight Harbor Authority issues an official order halting all outbound vessel movements at any time before 2028-11-12T18:00:00Z. Routine delays, pilot discretion, berth restrictions, or inbound-only limits do not qualify. Otherwise resolve OPERATING. This fixture is entirely fictional.

places.name: Northlight Watch House
places.description: Home base for the harbor expedition, market overview, and forecast commits.

places.name: Gullwing Signal Station
places.description: Fresh sea-state observations, wind warnings, and visibility reports.

places.name: Harbor Authority Office
places.description: Official traffic notices, public statements, and local reporting.

places.name: Tide Ledger Archive
places.description: Past suspension orders, channel incidents, and versioned authority records.

places.name: Tide Scholar's Loft
places.description: An independent desk for correlation, base-rate, and missing-evidence checks.

places.name: Lantern Quayside
places.description: A sheltered meeting point where agents exchange sourced observations.

places.name: Outer Breakwater Control
places.description: Direct vessel counts, channel-marker status, and pilot observations.

belief.rationale: The gale watch matters, but direct channel and vessel observations have not been checked.
Past suspensions usually required a conjunction of hazards rather than wind alone.
News reports may be repeating one warning, while authority language and field operations can diverge.

sources.title: Gullwing Gale and Sea-State Report 14:10Z
sources.excerpt: Gusts at the outer harbor are above the caution band and wave height is rising. Visibility remains acceptable. The fictional authority's automatic suspension threshold has not been crossed.

sources.title: Outbound Traffic Remains Open Pending 15:30 Review
sources.excerpt: Outbound traffic remains open under pilot discretion. The authority will review wind, wave, and channel-marker conditions at 15:30Z. This notice is not a guarantee that operations will continue.

sources.title: Outer Breakwater Departure Count 14:00-14:35Z
sources.excerpt: Three outbound vessels cleared the outer breakwater after 14:00Z. Two additional departures are delayed at pilot discretion; no harbor-wide suspension order is recorded in this interval.

sources.title: Case File: Thirty Gale-Watch Harbor Decisions
sources.excerpt: Eight of thirty comparable fictional gale-watch decisions produced outbound suspensions. Seven of those eight also involved an unavailable outer-channel marker or visibility below the authority minimum. The sample spans several harbor layouts.

sources.title: Northlight Closure Risk Rises With Gale Warning
sources.excerpt: A local wire report says suspension risk is rising. Its weather description comes from the Gullwing report and its operating status comes from the authority's 13:55Z notice; it adds no independent field observation.

sources.title: Pilots Expect Selective Delays, Not Harbor-Wide Suspension
sources.excerpt: The pilots association expects vessel-specific delays through the next review but does not expect a harbor-wide outbound halt on the current readings. The association does not issue authority orders.

sources.title: Outer Marker Inspection: Intermittent Signal
sources.excerpt: The outer-channel marker signal is intermittent and technicians are inspecting the power relay. Channel Control has not declared the marker unavailable.

sources.title: Outer Marker Inspection: Signal Restored and Verified
sources.excerpt: Technicians reseated the power relay. The outer-channel marker signal has remained stable through two verification cycles and is cleared for normal use.

claims.text: Outer-harbor gusts and wave height are above caution levels, but the automatic suspension threshold has not been crossed.
The harbor authority says outbound traffic remains open pending a 15:30 review.
Three outbound vessels cleared the breakwater after 14:00Z while two others were selectively delayed.
qualifiers: does not predict later authority action

claims.text: Eight of thirty comparable gale-watch decisions ended in suspension, usually when another channel or visibility hazard was also present.
The wire report says suspension risk is rising but derives its operational facts from two already available primary sources.
The pilots association expects selective delays rather than a harbor-wide outbound suspension on current readings.
The outer-channel marker signal was intermittent while technicians inspected its power relay.
The outer-channel marker signal was restored and passed two verification cycles by 15:05Z.

signals.headline: Gale conditions exceed the caution band
signals.summary: Fresh official conditions increase suspension risk, but the automatic threshold remains uncrossed.

signals.headline: Authority keeps outbound traffic open pending review
signals.summary: The current official operating status weighs against an immediate suspension but can change at 15:30Z.

reliability.label: verified_primary
reasons: Current status and next review time are explicit

signals.headline: Outbound vessels continue to clear the breakwater
signals.summary: Direct traffic counts show ongoing operations in a bounded interval, with selective delays rather than a general halt.

signals.headline: Historical suspensions usually required a second hazard
signals.summary: The conditional base rate makes wind-only extrapolation weak and focuses attention on visibility and channel-marker status.

reliability.label: derived
reasons: Conditional comparison rather than direct observation

signals.headline: Wire report frames suspension risk as rising
signals.summary: The framing is directionally negative, but it repeats the same upstream gale report and authority notice rather than adding independent evidence.

reliability.label: single_secondary
reasons: Must not be double-counted with its primary sources

signals.headline: Pilots expect selective delays rather than suspension
signals.summary: The primary stakeholder statement contradicts closure framing but cannot determine what the authority will order.

signals.headline: Outer-channel marker signal is intermittent
signals.summary: A possible second hazard raises suspension risk, although the marker was not declared unavailable and the inspection remained open.

signals.headline: Superseding notice verifies the channel marker
signals.summary: The restored and verified marker removes the strongest candidate second hazard from the historical conjunction.

reliability.label: verified_primary
reasons: Version link preserves the prior state

payload.rationale: Initial team prior before checking current harbor operations and source dependencies.

tern:observe_conditions:signal-station.dialogue: The gale report is fresh and cautionary, but it explicitly says the automatic suspension threshold has not been crossed.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Check whether outbound vessels are still clearing the outer breakwater.

tern:investigate:outer-breakwater.dialogue: Three vessels cleared the breakwater in the observed interval. Operations are still moving, though that does not settle what the authority may order later.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Verify the latest outer-channel marker inspection status.

tern:verify:outer-breakwater.dialogue: The marker signal is intermittent, which could become the second hazard the archive warns about. The notice is provisional and does not call it unavailable.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Look for a superseding Channel Control inspection notice.

brin:verify:outer-breakwater.dialogue: A newer official notice supersedes the intermittent-marker report: the relay was reseated and the signal passed two verification cycles. The older hazard signal should be stale.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Revisit the historical conjunction with the restored marker status.

cora:search_history:records-office.dialogue: Only eight of thirty gale-watch cases suspended outbound traffic, and nearly all eight had another channel or visibility hazard. Wind alone is a weak comparison.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Verify current channel-marker and visibility status before applying the base rate.

brin:verify:harbor-office.dialogue: The official notice keeps outbound traffic open only until the next review. It weighs against an immediate halt, not against every suspension before 18:00.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Compare closure reporting with primary stakeholder and authority statements.

brin:find_contradiction:harbor-office.dialogue: The closure headline repeats the same gale and authority sources, while pilots expect selective delays. That is a real disagreement, but neither item is an authority order.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Check whether the wire report adds independent evidence or only correlated framing.

professorFixture.answer: Do not count these as two independent warnings. The wire report explicitly derives its weather claim from the Gullwing report and adds framing rather than a new observation. Keep the official sea-state signal; treat the wire as correlated context, then seek channel status and the authority's actual decision rule.
assumptions: The wire report's disclosed attribution is complete.
The authority applies the fictional rule described in the market resolution criteria.
limitations: The selected signals do not include the 15:30 authority review.
The condition of the outer-channel marker may change quickly.
professorFixture.suggestedNextQuestion: Does the current marker status complete the historical two-hazard conjunction?

suggestedMission.objective: Retrieve the newest official outer-channel marker inspection notice.

resolutionFixture.resolutionNote: In the fictional fixture, the Northlight Harbor Authority issued an outbound suspension order at 17:42Z after visibility fell below its minimum.
